initial_budget_envelope_usd.rationale: Plan states $25M-$100M+ envelope verbatim (Selected Scenario, Expert Criticism). Low = stated minimum capital commitment ($25M); base = midpoint of stated range; high = upper end of stated range. Asymmetric upper-side because the source explicitly says '$100M or more'.

contingency_reserve_usd.rationale: Plan states $10M-$20M dedicated contingency buffer for legal defense, lobbying, and string-contention auctions (Project Plan mitigation). Low/high are the literal range endpoints.

string_contention_overrun_usd.rationale: Premortem stress-tests Financial risk overrun at $10M-$20M; Expert Criticism warns string contention 'rapidly depletes contingency funds'. High extended above stated range because Issue 2 / Risk 3 are described as compounding; low set below the stated band to allow the soft-landing case.

hsm_capex_usd.rationale: Executive Summary mandates $5M-$10M CapEx for internal HSM procurement. Bounds bracket the stated range exactly; midpoint as base.

regulatory_shock_cost_usd.rationale: Review Plan / Premortem stress-test states $5M-$10M extra defense cost on regulatory objection. Bounds match the stated range.

political_backlash_cost_usd.rationale: Risk 5 / Review Plan: political backlash causes $2M-$4M extra legal cost. Bounds match the stated range.

review_duration_months.rationale: Plan commits to 18-month delegation target; Risk 1 stress-tests 6-12 month regulatory delay (push to 24-30); Risk 5 stress-tests 3-6 month political delay. Base centered on plan commitment; high reflects realised execution under named delay shocks.

avg_registration_price_usd.rationale: Issue 2 sensitivity uses $75/domain as anchor and explores $50 downside. New gTLD wholesale prices typically $10-$200; .mars novelty supports premium upper end but adoption uncertainty bounds the low side.

y1_registration_volume.rationale: Issue 2 cites 3,000-5,000 domain volumes. New gTLD Year 1 volumes for niche/novelty TLDs cluster 1k-25k. .mars has no installed base, so low side weighted heavily; high reflects speculative upside from collector / defensive registrations.

annual_opex_usd.rationale: Issue 2 names '$5M-$10M OpEx' as the plan's stated assumption. Base centered on midpoint; high extends to $12M because Premortem warns proprietary sync tooling and 5-FTE staffing may push the high end past $10M.

string_contention_probability.rationale: ICANN historical contention rate for short single-word TLDs ~30-50%; .mars uniqueness and high public profile elevate probability. Risk 3 classifies likelihood as Medium and Risk 1 as High; bounds straddle both judgments.
